minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 — new rules for business signs take effect
minutes_briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has passed Bylaw 14834, called the Sign Bylaw 2026. This bylaw (a local law) replaces the old sign rules and sets new requirements for all signs on commercial and residential properties. It covers things like the size, placement, lighting, and type of signs you can put up. The new rules also change how you apply for a sign permit and what fees you pay. If your business has any outdoor signs — including awnings, sandwich boards, or window signs — you need to follow these new rules.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your current signs against the new Bylaw 14834 rules and apply for any needed permits before enforcement begins.
